other_observers: Found by Chuck Lowery and Gayle Campbell on the 11th. Also seen by Dave Leatherman on the 11th.; Chuck Lowery and Gayle Campbell
description: Crown and back rusty-brown. The sides of the head were quite gray. Distinct white eye-ring with rusty-brown streak behind eye. Two wing bands. Brown tail. Underparts whitish and unstreaked. Pink bill and pink legs.Behaviors: In with a large flock of Brewer's and Clay-colored Sparrows. Flying out into an open ag field to feed and then coming back to some large deciduous trees where it would sing quite distinctly from the Brewer'sCall: as described in Nat. Geo., "clear, plaintive whistles accelerating into a trill."Plumage: adult
